title: Exploring a Live AWS Web Application — EC2, PHP & MySQL
description: In this lab you will interact with a fully deployed AWS web application. You will use the browser-based PHP app to manage data, connect to the EC2 instance over SSH to explore the Linux environment and running services, and connect directly to the RDS MySQL database to run queries. No infrastructure changes are required — everything is already provisioned for you.
prerequisites: Basic familiarity with Linux command line (cd, ls, cat, systemctl)
Basic understanding of what a web server and database are
No AWS console access required for this lab

credentials.note: All values below are injected automatically by CloudLabs when the lab starts.

exercises.title: Exercise 2 — Insert and Manage Records via the App
Exercise 3 — Connect to EC2 via SSH
Exercise 4 — Explore the Web Server and Application Files
Exercise 5 — Connect to RDS MySQL and Run Queries
Exercise 6 — Check Running Services and Logs
Exercise 7 — Challenge: Add a New Record via MySQL CLI

validationChecks.description: At least one record exists in the users table
A record with name 'LabTest' exists in the users table
validationChecks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM webappdb.users WHERE name = 'LabTest'
